paper.abstract_en: Existence of Double-Logarithmic (DL) contributions to scattering amplitudes in QED was discovered by V.V. Sudakov in 1956 and total summation of DL contributions to electron-photon scattering resulted in appearance of famous Sudakov exponentials. Then, thanks to contributions of V.G. Gorshkov, V.N. Gribov, G.V. Frolov and L.N. Lipatov, the pattern of calculations in Double- Logarithmic Approximation (DLA) was constructed. Since then, DLA has become one of basic ways of describing various high-energy processes in the framework of QED, QCD and theory of EW interactions. In the present paper, we remind the history of DLA and present a brief overview of application of DLA to various objects like form factors, scattering amplitudes, DIS structure functions.
